mission.title: consul: [feature] HTTP/2 and gRPC health-checks support
mission.description: [feature] HTTP/2 and gRPC health-checks support. Requested by 27+ users.

steps.title: Check current consul deployment
steps.description:
Verify your consul version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul
helm list -n consul 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working consul installation.

steps.title: Review consul configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant consul configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul
kubectl get configmap -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=consul
```
Given the raising popularity of HTTP/2 and gRPC it'd be nice to be able to health-check such services natively with Consul.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [feature] HTTP/2 and gRPC health-checks support
Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul
kubectl get events -n consul --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[feature] HTTP/2 and gRPC health-checks support" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: This makes some minor tweaks to #3782 and adds documentation. I also renamed the `tls` config to `grpc_use_tls` to bind it closer to how it's used.
